name: onboarding
title: Commerce Onboarding API
description:
Onboarding platform facilitates onboarding a merchant onto GoDaddy's omni-channel products.

To onboard a merchant, various steps are taken to verify the owner information which permits access to payments, orders and GoDaddy products.

endpoints.description: Returns a list of existing onboarding applications

totalItems.description: The total number of applications found

totalPages.description: The total number of pages per grouped applications

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

items.title: Onboarding Application
items.description: This model represents the application which is use to begin the onboarding process

items.title: Individual
items.description: Business account owner or the person filling out the application (applicant)

address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)
address.description: An internationalized postal address. Maps to [AddressValidationMetadata](https://github.com/googlei18n/libaddressinput/wiki/AddressValidationMetadata) from Google's Address Data Service and HTML 5.1 [Autofilling form controls: the autocomplete attribute](https://www.w3.org/TR/html51/sec-forms.html#autofilling-form-controls-the-autocomplete-attribute).

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

email.description: A valid, internationalized email address. Note: Up to 64 characters are allowed before and 255 characters are allowed after the @ sign. However, the generally accepted maximum length for an email address is 254 characters. The pattern verifies that an unquoted @ sign exists.

phone.title: Phone
phone.description: The phone number, in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en).

countryCode.description: The country calling code (CC), in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the CC and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

nationalNumber.description: The national number, in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the country calling code (CC) and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

visitorId.description: The visitor id that was created during an anonymous visit

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: An abstraction of data to create a channel

externalChannelId.description: Unique ID what represents an external resource

externalChannelType.description: A term what represents the external channel id

metadata.description: Additional parameters to create a specific type of application

medium.description: The page or entity relating to this account. Ex. FacebookLP, Email, My Account

source.description: The point of origin that started this process

referralUrlId.description: The ID what was setup for a Distributor Merchant URL

applicationLevel.description: Current Application Level — i.e. BASIC, FULL

applicationStatus.description: The current status of the application

intent.description: Identifies the type of onboarding application that is being requested for the commerce account

products.description: List of GoDaddy product that can be added to the commerce account e.g., e.g., GD Payments, GD Debit card, GD Loans etc.

items.title: Product
items.description: GoDaddy product that can be added to the commerce account e.g., e.g., GD Payments, GD Debit card, GD Loans etc.

features.description: Optional capabilities/functionalities associated with GoDaddy offered Product

commerceAccountFeatures.description: Optional capabilities/functionalities associated with commerce account

endpoints.description: Creates an application and start the onboarding process

parameters.name: onboard
parameters.description: When false, the application will be created without processing

error-details.title: Error Details
error-details.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

value.description: The value of the field that caused the error.

location.description: The location of the field that caused the error. Value is `body`, `path`, or `query`.

issue.description: The unique fine-grained application-level error code.

description.description: The human-readable description for an issue. The description MAY change over the lifetime of an API, so clients MUST NOT depend on this value.

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

name.description: The human-readable, unique name of the error.

correlationId.description: Internal identifier used for correlation purposes.

message.description: The message that describes the error.

informationLink.description: The URI for detailed information related to this error for the developer.

details.description: An array of additional details about the error. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

items.title: Error Details
items.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

value.description: The value of the field that caused the error.

location.description: The location of the field that caused the error. Value is `body`, `path`, or `query`.

issue.description: The unique fine-grained application-level error code.

description.description: The human-readable description for an issue. The description MAY change over the lifetime of an API, so clients MUST NOT depend on this value.

links.description: An array of error-related HATEOAS links.

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

endpoints.description: Returns an onboarding application by id

The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

schema.title: Patch Request
schema.description: An array of JSON patch objects to apply partial updates to resources.

items.title: Patch
items.description: The JSON patch object to apply partial updates to resources.

path.description: The JSON pointer to the target document location at which to complete the operation.

value.description: The value to apply. The `remove` operation does not require a value.

from.description: The JSON pointer to the target document location from which to move the value. Required for the `move` operation.

schema.title: Onboarding Application
schema.description: This model represents the application which is use to begin the onboarding process

items.title: Individual
items.description: Business account owner or the person filling out the application (applicant)

address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)
address.description: An internationalized postal address. Maps to [AddressValidationMetadata](https://github.com/googlei18n/libaddressinput/wiki/AddressValidationMetadata) from Google's Address Data Service and HTML 5.1 [Autofilling form controls: the autocomplete attribute](https://www.w3.org/TR/html51/sec-forms.html#autofilling-form-controls-the-autocomplete-attribute).

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

email.description: A valid, internationalized email address. Note: Up to 64 characters are allowed before and 255 characters are allowed after the @ sign. However, the generally accepted maximum length for an email address is 254 characters. The pattern verifies that an unquoted @ sign exists.

phone.title: Phone
phone.description: The phone number, in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en).

countryCode.description: The country calling code (CC), in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the CC and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

nationalNumber.description: The national number, in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the country calling code (CC) and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

correlationId.description: Internal identifier used for correlation purposes.

message.description: The message that describes the error.

informationLink.description: The URI for detailed information related to this error for the developer.

details.description: An array of additional details about the error. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

items.title: Error Details
items.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

value.description: The value of the field that caused the error.

location.description: The location of the field that caused the error. Value is `body`, `path`, or `query`.

issue.description: The unique fine-grained application-level error code.

description.description: The human-readable description for an issue. The description MAY change over the lifetime of an API, so clients MUST NOT depend on this value.

links.description: An array of error-related HATEOAS links.

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

error-details.title: Error Details
error-details.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

name.description: The human-readable, unique name of the error.
